title: clerodendrum japonicum var. bethuneanum
intro: Clerodendrum japonicum var. bethuneanum, also known as the Glory Bower or Clerodendrum beth, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Japan. It is a woody climber with fragrant white flowers and attractive red berries, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is said to have antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Clerodendrum japonicum var. bethuneanum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and berries can be used to make a poultice for external use, providing relief from pain and inflammation.
